description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped rock with a rounded top and a relatively flat base, making it stable when placed on the ground. The upper surface is heavily covered in green moss, giving it a natural, weathered appearance and suggesting it has been exposed to moisture and shade for an extended period. The rock itself is primarily gray with patches of white and subtle brownish hues, visible where the moss coverage is thinner or absent. The texture appears rough and uneven, with small crevices and protrusions, especially along the sides. The moss layer is thickest on the top and thins out toward the lower edges, where the bare stone is more prominent. This rock would blend seamlessly into landscaped urban parks, gardens, or naturalized sidewalk edges.
description: A rounded, gray rock with a thick layer of green moss on top and rough, uneven surfaces. The moss is densest on the upper side, with bare stone visible around the edges.
description_view_0: The rock is seen from the front, showing a broad, moss-covered top and a rounded, stable base with some bare stone visible at the lower edge.
description_view_1: This side view highlights the sloping mossy surface and reveals more of the rock's irregular, rough stone texture along the right edge.
description_view_2: The rear view displays the flattest profile, with the moss layer appearing thinner and more of the gray stone exposed along the bottom.
description_view_3: From this angle, the rock's left side is visible, showing a mix of moss and bare stone, with a pronounced bulge and rough surface details.
